mnemonics: HENDR: Hierarchical, Entity-Relationship, Network, Dimensional, Relational (for common data model types)
Remember the Relational Model uses Tables, Rows, and Columns by thinking of TRC like a Television Remote Control - you use it to navigate data
keyPoints: A data model is a collection of concepts for describing data, relationships, semantics, and constraints.
The three main types of data models are conceptual (high-level, business-oriented), logical (detailed structure, DBMS-independent), and physical (implementation-specific).
The Relational Model, based on tables (relations), is the most widely used model in commercial DBMS.
commonMistakes: Confusing the Entity-Relationship Model (a conceptual/top-down design tool) with the Relational Model (a logical implementation model).
Assuming the Hierarchical and Network models are obsolete; they are still used in specific legacy systems (e.g., IMS, IDMS).
